LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS EDY MAGANA, DANY COLLI, EVELYN MARTINEZ'S SECOND MOTION TO APPROVE $1,000 IN PENALTIES SOUGHT AS PART OF A SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT PURSUANT TO THE PRIVATE ATTORNEYS GENERAL ACT [LABOR CODE § 2699(L)] IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED AND GOOD CAUSE SHOWN. APPROVAL OF THIS SETTLEMENT SHOULD NOT BE CONSTRUED AS THE COURT WILL APPROVE THE SETTLEMENT OF OTHER PAGA CLAIMS WHERE THE ENTIRETY OF THE 25% PORTION OF THE CIVIL PENALTIES IS PAID TO THE NAMED PLAINTIFFS. ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302)

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-08-2018 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Second Motion To Approve $1,000 In Penalties Sought As Part Of A Settlement Agreement Pursuant To The Private Attorneys General Act [Labor Code § 2699(L)]
Off Calendar Nov-02-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Un Kei Wu, Esq. 415-695-1000 of Liberation Law Group, P.C. for Plaintiff.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF EDY MAGANA, DANY COLLI, EVELYN MARTINEZ's Second Motion To Approve $1,000 In Penalties Sought As Part Of A Settlement Agreement Pursuant To The Private Attorneys General Act [Labor Code § 2699(L)]

Plaintiffs' second motion to approve $1,000 in penalties as part of a settlement agreement of a PAGA claim is granted. No opposition filed and good cause shown. Approval of this settlement should not be construed as the court will approve the settlement of other PAGA claims where the entirety of the 25% portion of the civil penalties is paid to the named plaintiffs.

Order is signed in Open Court. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D302)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-21-2017 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jan-23-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION To Approve $1,000 In Penalties Sought As Part Of A Settlement Agreement Pursuant To The Private Attorneys General Act [Labor Code § 2699(L)]
Off Calendar Sep-26-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Un Kei Wu of the law offices of Liberation Law Group PC (415-695-1000) appears as counsel for plaintiffs/moving parties

Attorney, Jonathan D. Martin of the law offices of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP (415-362-2580) appears as counsel for defendant/non-opposing party

The Court previously modified the tentative ruling and hearing was required. The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

PLAINTIFFS EDY MAGANA, DANY COLLI, and EVELYN MARTINEZ's MOTION To Approve $1,000 In Penalties Sought As Part Of A Settlement Agreement Pursuant To The Private Attorneys General Act [Labor Code § 2699(L)], is DENIED without prejudice to being refiled. Plaintiff is to include PAGA claim explanation.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Angie Diner, CSR No. 9581, e-mail: adiner@sftc.org, (L&M302)
